title: NBA Daily Fast Break
subtitle: March 16, 2026 – Catch up on last night's thrillers and today's matchups!

items.headline: Knicks Stage Epic 21-Point Comeback Against Short-Handed Warriors
items.summary: The New York Knicks orchestrated their biggest comeback of the season, rallying from a 21-point deficit to defeat a severely depleted Golden State Warriors squad 110-107. The Warriors were without key stars like Stephen Curry, Jimmy Butler III, and Draymond Green due to injuries and rest.

items.headline: Warriors Decimated by Injuries & Rest Day Ahead of Washington Clash
items.summary: The Golden State Warriors faced the Knicks without Stephen Curry (right knee pain), Jimmy Butler III, De'Anthony Melton, Draymond Green, Seth Curry, and Al Horford. The team faces another road game tonight in Washington.

items.headline: 76ers Hold Off Trail Blazers
items.summary: Quentin Grimes exploded for a season-high 31 points as the short-handed 76ers defeated the Trail Blazers. Deni Avdija led Portland with 25 points.

items.headline: DeRozan's Masterclass Leads Kings Past Jazz
items.summary: DeMar DeRozan dropped a season-high 41 points and dished out 11 assists, guiding the Kings to a 116-111 victory over the Jazz. Cody Williams paced Utah with 34 points.

items.headline: Knicks Rally to Stun Warriors
items.summary: Jalen Brunson's 30 points and 9 assists fueled a monumental 21-point comeback for the Knicks against the injury-riddled Warriors.

items.headline: Thunder Extend Win Streak Over Timberwolves
items.summary: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander scored 20 points, pushing the Thunder to their eighth consecutive win with a 116-103 victory over the Timberwolves.

items.headline: Giannis Nears Triple-Double in Bucks Win
items.summary: Giannis Antetokounmpo delivered 31 points, 14 rebounds, and 8 assists to lead the Bucks past the Pacers 134-123.

items.headline: Mavericks Dominate Cavaliers
items.summary: Rookie Cooper Flagg impressed with 27 points and 10 assists as Dallas cruised to a 111-90 win over Cleveland.

items.headline: Rockets Edge Pelicans in Close Contest
items.summary: Kevin Durant hit the go-ahead shot to secure a tight 107-105 win for the Rockets against the Pelicans, despite Dejounte Murray's 35 points for New Orleans.

items.headline: Pelicans Host Mavericks
items.summary: The New Orleans Pelicans clash with the Dallas Mavericks tonight at the Smoothie King Center.

items.headline: Rockets Battle Lakers
items.summary: The Houston Rockets welcome the Los Angeles Lakers to the Toyota Center in an anticipated Western Conference matchup.

items.headline: Warriors Face Wizards in Washington
items.summary: The Golden State Warriors continue their road trip with a game against the Washington Wizards.

items.headline: Trail Blazers Head to Brooklyn
items.summary: The Portland Trail Blazers continue their East Coast swing against the Brooklyn Nets.

items.headline: DeMar DeRozan's Scoring Spree
items.summary: Sacramento's DeMar DeRozan erupted for a season-high 41 points and added 11 assists in the Kings' win over the Jazz.

items.headline: Grimes Leads Short-Handed Sixers
items.summary: Quentin Grimes delivered a season-high 31 points to power the Philadelphia 76ers to a victory against the Trail Blazers.

items.headline: Deni Avdija's Strong Outing
items.summary: Israeli forward Deni Avdija led the Portland Trail Blazers with 25 points in their matchup against the Philadelphia 76ers.

items.headline: Giannis Dominates for Bucks
items.summary: Giannis Antetokounmpo was nearly unstoppable with 31 points, 14 rebounds, and 8 assists in Milwaukee's win over Indiana.

items.headline: Dejounte Murray's Big Scoring Night
items.summary: Despite a loss, Dejounte Murray poured in 35 points for the New Orleans Pelicans against the Houston Rockets.

items.headline: Rookie Sensation Cooper Flagg Shines
items.summary: Dallas Mavericks rookie Cooper Flagg impressed with 27 points and 10 assists in a win over the Cavaliers.

items.headline: Rookie Edgecombe's Third Double-Double
items.summary: 76ers rookie VJ Edgecombe recorded his third double-double of the season with 18 points and 12 rebounds against Portland.

items.headline: Cody Williams' Career High
items.summary: Utah Jazz forward Cody Williams notched a career-best 34 points in the contest against the Sacramento Kings.

items.headline: Killian Hayes Signs Extension with Sacramento
items.summary: Guard Killian Hayes has reportedly signed a contract extension with the Sacramento Kings, cementing his future with the team.

items.headline: Max Strus Returns for Cavaliers
items.summary: Cleveland's Max Strus made his season debut after recovering from left foot surgery, contributing 24 points in his return.

items.headline: Thunder's Winning Streak Extends to Eight Games
items.summary: The Oklahoma City Thunder continue their impressive run, extending their winning streak to eight games with a victory over the Minnesota Timberwolves.

items.headline: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander Extends 20-Point Streak
items.summary: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander maintained his remarkable streak of scoring at least 20 points, a record-breaking achievement, in the Thunder's latest win.
